channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Finally. $221 million flow into Bitcoin ETFs, ending a painful 10-day outflow streak
item.description: Spot ETFs had their strongest inflow day in two months, driven by funds other than BlackRock’s IBIT....

item.title: Ether and solana extend gains as a short squeeze lifts bitcoin toward $62,000
item.description: Bearish traders lost $281 million in liquidations over 24 hours, nearly double the longs, as bitcoin pushed to its strongest level in two weeks. Ether is up almost 10% on the week and solana nearly 19...
item.dc:creator: Coindesk

item.title: Claude Fable 5 isn’t permanently leaving subscriptions, Anthropic says
item.description: Anthropic says Claude Fable 5 won't be accessible via Claude subscriptions after July 7, but it's not a permanent change, and the company expects the model to return outside the usage-based plan soon....

item.title: Google Disrupts NetNut Residential Proxy Network Spanning 2 Million Home Devices

Google has significantly degraded NetNut, one of the biggest networks that turns home devices into rented relays for other people's traffic.
Working with the FBI, Lumen, and others, Google's Threat I...

item.title: Ransomware Groups Turn to Citrix Bleed 2, BYOVD, and Supply Chain Credentials
item.description:

item.title: How tokenized stocks fail as collateral even when the stock price does not move

item.title: Anthropic Restores Claude Fable 5 After U.S. Lifts Jailbreak-Linked Export Controls
item.description: Anthropic is putting Claude Fable 5 back online worldwide. On June 30, the U.S. Commerce Department lifted the export controls it had imposed on Fable and its more tightly controlled sibling Mythos 5 ...

item.title: Azure CLI Password Spray Hits at Least 78 Microsoft Accounts in 81M+ Attempts
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a "massive, ongoing, automated password spray attack" aimed at Microsoft's Azure command-line interface (CLI), compromising dozens of accounts in the process. ...
